# AES Encryption/Decryption Module

Implement a symmetric encryption module using the AES-256-CBC algorithm that can encrypt and decrypt string messages.

## Capabilities

### Encrypt and decrypt with AES-256-CBC

Use a 32-byte key and a 16-byte initialization vector (IV) to encrypt plaintext, then decrypt ciphertext back to the original message.

- Encrypting `"Hello, World!"` with a 32-byte key and 16-byte IV, then decrypting the result returns `"Hello, World!"` [@test](./tests/round-trip.test.js)
- The encrypted output is a hex-encoded string (different from the original plaintext) [@test](./tests/encrypt-format.test.js)
- Encrypting the same message with different IVs produces different ciphertexts [@test](./tests/different-iv.test.js)
- Decrypting ciphertext with the wrong key throws an error or returns garbage [@test](./tests/wrong-key.test.js)

## Implementation

[@generates](./src/index.js)

## API

```javascript { #api }
/**
 * Encrypt a plaintext string using AES-256-CBC.
 * @param {string} plaintext - The text to encrypt
 * @param {Buffer} key - 32-byte encryption key
 * @param {Buffer} iv - 16-byte initialization vector
 * @returns {string} Hex-encoded ciphertext
 */
export function encrypt(plaintext, key, iv);

/**
 * Decrypt a hex-encoded ciphertext using AES-256-CBC.
 * @param {string} ciphertext - Hex-encoded ciphertext
 * @param {Buffer} key - 32-byte decryption key (must match encryption key)
 * @param {Buffer} iv - 16-byte initialization vector (must match encryption IV)
 * @returns {string} The decrypted plaintext as a UTF-8 string
 */
export function decrypt(ciphertext, key, iv);
```

## Dependencies { .dependencies }

### crypto-browserify { .dependency }

Provides browser-compatible implementation of Node.js's `crypto` module, including createCipheriv and createDecipheriv for symmetric encryption.

[@satisfied-by](crypto-browserify)
